perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, Rogers may terminate this Agreement, at any time, upon sixty (60) days' prior written notice to Licensor.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, Rogers may terminate this Agreement, at any time, upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to Licensor.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the notice period required for Rogers to terminate the agreement. Section 18 states a sixty (60) day notice period, while Section 7 states that after the Initial Term or the Renewal Term, each of Licensor and Rogers shall have the right, on sixty (60) days' prior written notice, to terminate this Agreement, as so extended. This perturbation creates an ambiguity as to what the correct notice period is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement (the "Initial Term") shall commence as of the Effective Date and, unless earlier terminated in accordance with this Agreement, shall terminate on June 30, 2010. At Rogers' option, this Agreement shall renew for a subsequent term of two (2) years on the terms and conditions herein (the "Renewal Term"). Notwithstanding the foregoing, if, at the expiry of this Agreement following the Initial Term or the Renewal Term (if any), as applicable, Licensor and Rogers have not executed a new agreement governing the VOD distribution and exhibition of Licensed Programs and Rogers (or its permitted assigns) continues to distribute and exhibit Licensed Programs on the ROD Service following such expiry, such continued distribution and exhibition shall be governed by the terms of this Agreement in effect at the time of expiry, except that each of Licensor and Rogers shall have the right, on sixty (60) days' prior written notice, to terminate this Agreement, as so extended. For greater certainty, all references to "Term" in this Agreement shall include the Initial Term, the Renewal Term (if any) and any period during which this Agreement continues following its expiry in accordance with this Section.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notice period required for Rogers to terminate the agreement from sixty (60) days to thirty (30) days. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that after the Initial Term or the Renewal Term, each of Licensor and Rogers shall have the right, on sixty (60) days' prior written notice, to terminate this Agreement. This creates an inconsistency as to what the correct notice period is, fulfilling the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Licensor shall provide to Rogers, no later than February 28 in each year, a sworn statement of a senior officer of Licensor, or a certificate of the auditors of Licensor, confirming that, during the immediately preceding calendar year, Licensor did not enter into such an agreement or series of agreements or, if it did enter into such agreement(s), confirming the effective date thereof and identifying the terms contained therein that are more favourable than those contained in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensor shall provide to Rogers, no later than March 15 in each year, a sworn statement of a senior officer of Licensor, or a certificate of the auditors of Licensor, confirming that, during the immediately preceding calendar year, Licensor did not enter into such an agreement or series of agreements or, if it did enter into such agreement(s), confirming the effective date thereof and identifying the terms contained therein that are more favourable than those contained in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the deadline for Licensor to provide a sworn statement or auditor's certificate. Originally, the deadline was February 28, now it is March 15. This contradicts Section 26, Notices, where all other notices have specific timelines and the expectation is the same deadline is followed. Now there are two conflicting deadlines, creating ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any notice, request, demand, consent or other communication (collectively a "Notice") provided or permitted hereunder shall be in writing and given by personal delivery (against receipt), or sent by registered mail (against receipt) postage prepaid, or transmitted by facsimile (provided that a hard copy is immediately sent by registered mail), addressed to the other party for which it is intended at its address below: To Rogers: Rogers Cable Communications Inc. 333 Bloor Street East Toronto, Ontario M4W 1G9 Attention: Vice-President, General Manager, Television FAX: (416) 935-4600 with a copy to: Rogers Communications Inc. 333 Bloor Street East Toronto, Ontario M4W 1G9 Attention: Vice-President, General Counsel FAX: (416) 935-3548 To Licensor: Sebastien Perioche Eurocinema 1395 Brickell Ave., Suite 800, Miami, FL 33131, 305-529-6220 FAX: 305-529-6201 Any Notice given pursuant to this Agreement shall make specific reference to this Agreement. Any Notice so given shall be deemed to have been received on the date on which it was delivered in person, or transmitted, if delivered or transmitted on a day, other than a Saturday, on which the Toronto-Dominion Bank in Toronto is open for business (a "Business Day") during normal business hours of the recipient and, if not so delivered or transmitted, on the next Business Day or, if sent by registered mail, on the fifth (5t h) Business Day thereafter; provided, however, that either party may change its address and/or facsimile number for purposes of receipt of any such communication by giving ten (10) days prior written notice of such change to the other party in the manner prescribed above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the deadline for providing the sworn statement/certificate from February 28 to March 15. The contradicted text (Section 26) establishes a general procedure for notices, suggesting a standardized timeline. By changing the deadline in the modified text, it directly conflicts with the established practice of using similar timelines as outlined in Section 26. This introduces an inconsistency as to which deadline applies to the sworn statement, creating a strong contradiction.
